creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
Based on test results a few weeks ago (to freebsd-test <https://lists.freebsd.org/archives/freebsd-test/>), I believe that in some cases: 

* there's no return to sender when an email is not delivered.

I performed those tests, because one of my 2nd November responses to <https://lists.freebsd.org/archives/freebsd-questions/2022-October/002152.html> was (apparently) not delivered; not received. There followed some flaring of tempers, which could have been avoided if my email had been received by the person who asked the question.

Today at 13:39:59 +0000 I sent an e-mail in response to <https://lists.freebsd.org/archives/freebsd-hackers/2023-January/001749.html>, my response is not yet archived (it's now 15:05), so I assume that it was rejected.
